title: Group messaging security
abstract: Disclosed herein is a method for enabling a first recipient of media in which at least a portion of the media is concealed to render the concealed media when the first recipient has permission, the method comprising receiving, when the first recipient does not have permission to view a first version of media, a second version of the media deriving from the first version, wherein at least a portion of the first version of the media is concealed in the second version, the second version of the media further comprising a distribution list identifying one or more further recipients the second version has been sent to; and, accessing, when the first recipient has permission to observe the first version of the media, data linked to the at least a portion of the first version of the media that enables the first recipient to descramble the at least a portion of the first version of the media; wherein granting the first recipient permission comprises monitoring a location of each of the further recipients and granting permission to the first recipient to observe the first version of the media based on the monitoring.
claims: Claims Claims: 1 . A method for enabling a first recipient of media in which at least a portion of the media is concealed to render the concealed media when the first recipient has permission, the media being sent to one or more further recipients, the method comprising: receiving, when the first recipient does not have permission to view a first version of media, a second version of the media deriving from the first version, wherein at least a portion of the first version of the media is concealed in the second version; and, accessing, when the first recipient has permission to observe the first version of the media, data linked to the at least a portion of the first version of the media that enables the first recipient to descramble the at least a portion of the first version of the media; the method further comprising: granting permission to the first recipient to observe the first version of the media based on a location of the first recipient and a location of one or more of the further recipients. 2. A method according to claim 1 , wherein the concealed portion is an encrypted portion, the data linked to the at least a portion of the first version corresponding to a decryption key corresponding to the encryption key used to generate the encrypted portion. 3. A method according to claim 1 , wherein the concealed portion is an obscured portion, the data linked to the at least a portion of the first version being data representative of the least a portion of the first version of the media. 4. A method according to one of the previous claims, wherein granting permission further comprises: determining that at least a predefined number of the further recipients are within proximity of the first recipient. 5. A method according to the previous claim 4, wherein determining that a further recipient is within proximity of the first recipient comprises: detecting an identifier of the further recipient using a proximity range connection. 6. A method according to one of the previous claims, wherein granting permission further comprises monitoring the location of the one or more of the further recipients. 7. A method according to the previous claim 6, wherein monitoring of the location of a further recipient comprises: determining that the location of a further recipient matches a predefined criterion. 8. A method according to one of the previous claims, wherein granting permission further comprises: generating a challenge for the first recipient; granting permission when the challenge is succeeded by the first recipient. 9. A method according to one of the previous claims, wherein the second media further comprising a duration over which the monitoring of the location is carried out. 10. A method according to one of the previous claims, wherein associated with the second version of the media is a distribution list identifying the one or more further recipients the second version of the media has been sent to. 1 1 . A method according to claim 10, wherein the sender of the message is part of the distribution list. 12. A method according to one of the previous claims, wherein granting the permission further comprises verifying that the first recipient is in possession of a key linked to the second version of the media 13. A method according to one of the previous claims, wherein granting permission is discontinued based on a further location of the first recipient and a further location of one or more of the further recipients. 14. A user device comprising a memory and a processor, the memory storing computer readable code operable by the processor to perform the method of any one of claims 1 to 12. 15. A user device comprising a memory and a processor, the memory storing computer readable code operable by the processor to: receive, when the first recipient does not have permission to view a first version of media, a second version of the media deriving from the first version, wherein at least a portion of the first version of the media is concealed in the second version; and, access, when the first recipient has permission to observe the first version of the media, data linked to the at least a portion of the first version of the media that enables the first recipient to descramble the at least a portion of the first version of the media; the computer readable code being further operable by the processor to: grant permission to the first recipient to observe the first version of the media based on a location of the first recipient and a location of one or more of the further recipients. 16. A user device according to claim 15, wherein the concealed portion is an encrypted portion, the data linked to the at least a portion of the first version corresponding to a decryption key corresponding to the encryption key used to generate the encrypted portion. 17. A user device according to claim 15, wherein the concealed portion is an obscured portion, the data linked to the at least a portion of the first version being data representative of the least a portion of the first version of the media. 18. A user device according to any one of claims 15 to 17, wherein the computer readable code is further operable by the processor when granting permission to: determine that at least a predefined number of the further recipients are within proximity of the first recipient. 19. A user device according to claim 18, wherein the computer readable code is further operable by the processor when determining that a further recipient is within proximity of the first recipient comprises to: detect an identifier of the further recipient using a proximity range connection. 20. A user device according to any one of claims 15 to 19, wherein the computer readable code is further operable by the processor when granting permission to monitor the location of the one or more of the further recipients. 21 . A user device according to claim 20, wherein the computer readable code is further operable by the processor when monitoring the location of a further recipient to: determine that the location of a further recipient matches a predefined criterion. 22. A user device according to any one of claims 15 to 21 , wherein the computer readable code is further operable by the processor when granting permission to: generate a challenge for the first recipient; grant permission when the challenge is succeeded by the first recipient. 23. A user device according to claim to any one of claims 15 to 22, wherein the second media further comprising a duration over which the monitoring of the location is carried out. 24. Computer readable media implementable on a computer system and operable, in use, to perform the method of any one of claims 1 to 13. 25. Computer readable media implementable on a computer system and operable, in use, to: receive, when the first recipient does not have permission to view a first version of media, a second version of the media deriving from the first version, wherein at least a portion of the first version of the media is concealed in the second version; and, access, when the first recipient has permission to observe the first version of the media, data linked to the at least a portion of the first version of the media that enables the first recipient to descramble the at least a portion of the first version of the media; the computer readable media being further operable to: grant permission to the first recipient to observe the first version of the media based on a location of the first recipient and a location of one or more of the further recipients. 26. A computer readable media according to claim 25, wherein the concealed portion is an encrypted portion, the data linked to the at least a portion of the first version corresponding to a decryption key corresponding to the encryption key used to generate the encrypted portion. 27. A computer readable media according to claim 25, wherein the concealed portion is an obscured portion, the data linked to the at least a portion of the first version being data representative of the least a portion of the first version of the media. 28. A computer readable media according to any one of claims 25 to 27, wherein the computer readable media is further operable to when granting permission: determine that at least a predefined number of the further recipients are within proximity of the first recipient. 29. A computer readable media according to claim 28, wherein the computer readable media is further operable to perform when determining that a further recipient is within proximity of the first recipient: detect an identifier of the further recipient using a proximity range connection. 30. A computer readable media according to any one of claims 25 to 29, wherein the computer readable media is further operable to, when granting permission, monitor the location of the one or more of the further recipients. 31 . A computer readable media according to claim 30, wherein the computer readable media is further operable to, when monitoring the location of a further recipient: determine that the location of a further recipient matches a predefined criterion. 32. A computer readable media according to any one of claims 25 to 31 , wherein the computer readable media is further operable to when granting permission: generate a challenge for the first recipient; grant permission when the challenge is succeeded by the first recipient. 33. A computer readable media according to any one of claims 25 to 32, wherein the second media further comprising a duration over which the monitoring of the location is carried out.
